item.description:
====

Hi, everyone, Klaus again,. The other day a friend said that. people in upper class must be bad and evil. I’m sure that’s what most people believe. After all, most of us are at the bottom of the social ladder. We can only imagine what the upper class is like. Thus, conspiracy theories are easily spread. Since we are at the bottom, we tend to accept that the upper class is more evil. This issue is somewhat similar to the millennium-old debate between elitism and populism. Today let’s talk about it. Let's start with my own personal experience. In the US, white people are relatively in the upper class. I believe that those of Chinese descent living in the US will agree that. we seldom face racism from white people in our daily life,. and that racism comes more from ethnic minorities. White people are generally very polite and friendly. Also, they are generally law-abiding. White people generally cannot be bribed. You’d have to bribe someone else. In the US, white landlords generally do not accept cash.

Landlords of ethnic minorities (especially Chinese landlords). generally only take cash to evade taxes. It makes sense that those receiving a better education. and growing up in a quality environment become better people. Of course, we do not have access to high society,. so we can’t draw conclusions based on our own experience alone. History is the best teacher. History can always give us answers. Look at China’s millennia-old history,. excepting foreign invasions such as the Yuan and Qing dynasties,. the turmoil of the lower class has always caused the greatest harm. Every peasant rebellion in history was basically by bandits,. who robbed, killed and burned everywhere they went. This anti-China netizen summed it up pretty well,. “Mass murderers of the Chinese were mostly from the lower class.”. Huang Chao, the leader of the peasant rebellion in the Tang Dynasty,. after failing in the imperial exam, wrote the following poem,. “On the eighth day of the ninth month when autumn arrives,. my flowers will bloom whilst a hundred flowers wither. My fragrance will penetrate Chang'an and reach the sky,. and the entire city will be wearing golden armor.”.

Later, he indeed led a peasant rebellion and slaughtered the city. Li Zicheng, the leader of the peasant rebellion in the late Ming Dynasty,. Zhang Xianzhong, who composed the poem "Seven Killings”,. and the Taiping leader Hong Xiuquan, who also failed in the imperial exam, etc,. all caused mass extermination of the population during their rebellions. The Chinese Communist Party also belonged to this category. In the early decades of its ruling. the CCP caused tens of millions to hundreds of millions of deaths in China. The rise of Communist China was, in essence, a peasant uprising at the bottom. The only difference is that it was augmented by foreign forces (the Communist International). Thus, in the history book written by the CCP,. all those mass murderers in history are talked about positively. Memorials have been built for them everywhere. as a tool to teach the people patriotism. By contrast, unrest caused by the upper class usually didn’t involve mass killings of the people. After all, the upper class is relatively more educated and cultured. They consider their reputation and have a moral compass. But when peasants do evil, morals can be entirely thrown out of the window. For instance, during the Anti-Rightist Movement and the Cultural Revolution,. as instructed by Mao Zedong, 0.5% of the nation was labelled as “rightists”.

When Mao’s order was executed, perhaps that number became 5%,. aka, the historical expansion of the Anti-Rightist Movement. Even though the Central Committee had rules and regulations,. if you so much as rolled your eyes in front of your superior,. you would be labelled as a “rightist”. Besides, the central government was not likely to teach you how to kill people. However, people at the bottom were killing each other in all kinds of colourful ways. Nowadays, regarding how the Chinese were tortured during the Japanese invasion,. many Chinese people gnash their teeth in hatred. However, the Chinese have done a far better job at torturing Chinese people. Thus, the idea that people at the bottom are kinder,. that there is a paradise somewhere in the world. where a group of innocent people, untainted by society, dance together,. where a kind little girl pick mushrooms in the mountains,. is merely a fairy tale. I myself grew up in a rural area. I’ve seen siblings waging wars on one another, nobody wanting to look after their aging parents. People at the bottom can fight to the death over scraps. How could people of the lower class be viewed as kinder? Only with material satisfaction can we enrich our spiritual life.

Elitism and populism are both extreme views. But if I have to pick a side, I will stand in the middle slightly in favor of the elite. Historical facts, logic, and my personal experience have taught us that. people are inherently evil (that's why we need the rule of law, not the rule of man). Everyone is evil, but generally speaking, the upper class is better. Of course, fake snobs like Xi (who only graduated from primary school) are not part of this discussion. In a transparent society, the upper class is generally better. Now let's delve deeper into this issue,. which reminds me of the question posed by Musk earlier. Is capital more exploitative or power more exploitative? Nowadays, many Chinese people are always criticizing capital. Let’s discuss. The pursuit of profit is an essential part of the definition of capitalism. In other words, capitalism is profit-seeking. Capital and power are both scythes. In front of the two, we ordinary people are all lambs to be slaughtered. Capital seeks to maximize profit and will at least leave you alive. Power which is ever-hungry, however, will skin you alive if you dare to resist. Why is communism successful in China? Of course, there are many reasons,.

one of being that for thousands of years,. China’s autocratic society has always emphasized agriculture and suppressed business. This coincides with communism, whose ultimate goal is to eliminate capitalist private ownership. Cracking down on commerce and eliminating capital both enable those in power to better exploit. Commerce and capital create equality, expand freedom and increase social mobility. Commerce was born out of bartering. It is about equality, mutual benefit and the pursuit of a win-win situation,. everything the small-peasant mentality produced by China's millennia-old autocracy is not. Peasants crave self-sufficiency, no collaboration and no exchange. This has led to the selfishness of the entire nation,. who lacks cooperation and win-win awareness, not to mention the spirit of contract. In addition, capital must be mobile. Without mobility, there is no capital. Thus, China has been restricting the movement of goods and people for thousands of years. In ancient China, people needed a permit to leave a county. Most people could spend their entire lifetime never leaving their birthplace. If capitalism shears, then power butchers. By contrast, the flow of capital promotes the flow of social class. That's why China's reform and opening up has created a large middle class. That's why there are so many stories of people. building their wealth from the ground up through hard work.

For millennia, there was no class mobility in China. Once a slave, always a slave. The poor could never get ahead in life. Therefore, power and capital are essentially in competition with each other. It can even be said that the entire history of civilization is a struggle and compromise between power and capital. Many Chinese people do not realize this. Many people in China condemn capital. So many people hold the view that capitalism has hijacked power. If you feel that the social ladder is harder and harder to climb,. that is because capital has lost to power,. and has become the puppet and scythe of power. The core of a capital-led market economy is competition. Capital's exploitation will at least give you options. Power, however, forces you to buy and sell. You do not even have the power to say no. In the future, as the totalitarian dictatorship in China strengthens,. I believe more and more people will feel the change. from being exploited by capital to being exploited by power. Well, that's it for this video. Like, comment, subscribe, and share.

This is Klaus. See you next time.

====

item.title: 中國疫情大爆炸：白紙革命該不該背鍋？

item.itunes:author: 想做教育家的Klaus
item.itunes:summary:
====

Hello, this is Klaus. Do you regularly follow the news on China? It is filled with plot twists. A while ago, the White Paper Revolution broke out in various cities and universities in China. Many parts of the country protested against the draconian COVID measures. Then, COVID restrictions were lifted all over China. Xi Jinping says China's easing of COVID restrictions is a sign of great governance. But soon after, COVID cases surged all over the country. In many places, patients are unable to enter hospitals,. and are being treated in the corridors of hospitals and even on the side of the road. Crematoriums are overwhelmed, and have to put a daily limit on corpses. Some people simply dropped dead bodies at the entrance of the crematorium and left. Some say they have to spend tens of thousands of dollars. for their dead relatives to be moved to the front of the queue for cremation. How ridiculous and tragic! Recently, many celebrities have got COVID and even died as a result,. not just because a new variant of the virus is fast spreading. China’s vaccine cannot prevent COVID infections at all. Hu Xijin, the famous CCP mouthpiece, has also been infected.

Last year, Hu said that someone of his stature could have received an imported vaccine,. but as a patriot he chose China’s vaccine. He accidentally leaked the whereabouts of 100 million doses of Pfizer vaccine China imported last year. I wonder if he has any regrets now. The virus is spreading at such an astonishing rate that. many suspect that the virus is being set loose on purpose. Some say there are even COVID cases in remote and uninhabited areas,. and believe a government plane must’ve dropped the poison from the sky. as punishment for protesting against the lockdowns. But I believe if China really wants the public to know how serious COVID is,. there are better options. After all, the government decides what the infection rate and death toll are. Public opinion can be manipulated by the media, so there is no need to poison the masses. Moreover, China is still trying to cover up the epidemic, as usual. The number of reported COVID cases and deaths is abnormally low. A netizen said, "I called my mom this morning to tell her my family of three all have COVID. The old lady was shocked, ‘I heard that there were 3 cases in Beijing yesterday. They are all in your family?’”. Thus, it is unlikely that the Chinese government dropped the virus from the sky.

On the contrary, Chinese officials are now downplaying the danger of COVID. Someone working in the system has received a notice from their superiors,. “We ask all medical professionals to put the interests of the country and the health of the people first. and be COVID positive in an orderly manner.”. Previously, they shut down the country as much as possible. Today, they simply let the virus be. China's official attitude towards COVID has had a 180-degree reversal. Zhong Nanshan the Internet celebrity has also stopped insisting on Zero COVID,. saying that getting COVID is now equivalent to getting vaccinated. People with COVID used to be shunned and discriminated against. Nowadays, being COVID positive has become the new "positive energy". Where there are jobs available, those who’ve been or are COVID positive are given priority. Despite the major plot twist, discrimination remains. There have also been variants like the "foodie strain" and "study strain" that people crave. Many ask the patients if they could visit and voluntarily get infected. In order to whitewash COVID, the media even downplays the pandemic in the US. After years of propaganda, everyone in China knows that there have been over a million deaths in the US. Now the government is saying that the virus is not that deadly,. and that there actually haven’t been that many deaths in the US.

It is insane how fast the Chinese media changes its narrative. China is now experiencing the chaos and tragedy of a pandemic outbreak. This has led to a change in attitudes toward the White Paper Revolution. Many people have started to ridicule and attack the White Paper Revolution. If we want to re-evaluate the White Paper Revolution,. then let's analyze it objectively. The White Paper Revolution definitely has to do with the easing of COVID restrictions. The two have a cause-and-effect relationship. The nationwide easing of restrictions must be the order of the central government,. that is, the order of Xi Jinping. Previously, when some places protested against the COVID measures,. the local government lifted the restrictions on its own. As a result, local officials were all fired. for being inconsistent with the Party Central Committee. Nowadays, the order to ease restrictions must have come from Xi himself. Previously, Xi Jinping commented on the white paper protest,. saying that young people are frustrated with the COVID measures. The young people demanded that the CCP and Xi step down,. which really had a great impact on Xi Jinping.

So it can be said that the white paper campaign led to China easing its COVID restrictions. But can you blame the pandemic surge on the White Paper Campaign? Certainly not! Some say that the lockdowns ended prematurely. since there is no medicine or effective vaccine,. and therefore the white paper campaign should take the blame. Following that logic, if China had waited a little longer, would it be ready then? Would China have a strong medical foundation and a better vaccine then? Let's not talk about the cost of continuing the lockdowns. Which one kills more people, the lockdown or the virus itself? Besides, it's been three years since the outbreak. Has China done anything to prepare for the end of the lockdowns? I don’t think China would be ready if given a decade in lockdown. Before ending lockdowns, foreign nations actively made preparations. Those who live in Japan, Europe and the US know this. There are free supplies and medications available for those with COVID. They only need to stay home and enjoy income subsidies. China has previously mocked foreign countries for "giving up fighting COVID". In fact, in foreign countries, the people can sit back and relax while the government works hard fighting the pandemic. In today’s China, the government simply gives up, leaving the people for dead.

The fangcang hospitals, built with taxpayers’ money, are completely useless against the virus spiraling out of control. There is an acute shortage of medical resources. Cold and fever medicine are out of stock. Reportedly, the raw materials for the antipyretic drug, ibuprofen, are being confiscated by the government. Rumor has it that people spend tens of thousands of dollars on antipyretic drugs. COVID causes high fever, and a lack of medication has led to deaths. Even children of the “secretly cheering” diplomat had no fever-reducing medicine,. and received 4 tablets from neighbor Hu Xijin. The average Chinese citizen is not worse off, right? So, is the White Paper Revolution to blame? This netizen has a nice metaphor,. “A car is running towards a road that leads to a cliff. As it gets closer and closer to the cliff,. the driver decides to turn around. Usually, a car would first slow down and then slowly turn around. But this driver is quite peculiar. He steps on the gas and turns the steering wheel furiously. As expected, the car flips. The people in the car are blaming it on those who ask the driver to turn around.

Surely the car wouldn’t have flipped over if it had gone straight ahead.”. As you can see, it is laughable to blame the pandemic on the White Paper Revolution. When you are not sick, they force PCR tests and vaccines on you. They even invade your home, kick your door in, and do it for free! When you are really sick and really need medicine, where are they? You can't even buy medicine with money. If the government really cared about you and was really serving the people,. it should be invading your house to give you drugs and supplies, right? China has not gained any experience in epidemic prevention in the past three years. The so-called “great nation fighting COVID”, “taking responsibilities”, “systemic advantage”,. “in-person command”, “dynamic zero COVID”, fangcang hospitals, lockdowns and isolation, etc.,. everything that China invented in relation to COVID. has been proven to be useless and garbage by now. It is pure nonsense, and over a billion people have been played like monkeys. Looking back at these past three years of COVID,. is it any different from the Great Leap Forward? You can do a detailed comparison if you’re interested. Well, that's it for today's video! Like, subscribe, comment and share!

This is Klaus. See you next time.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cF_LS1v2jZg

item.title: 中国各地抗议口号大合集：一觉醒来，中国翻天了！让人热泪盈眶
item.itunes:title: 中国各地抗议口号大合集：一觉醒来，中国翻天了！让人热泪盈眶
item.itunes:author: 想做教育家的Klaus
item.itunes:summary:
====

University students in China are protesting in a way that has rarely been seen since June 4, 1989. Now the people of China have banded together. They are not to be messed with. Mess with them, and there will be hell to pay. End COVID testing! End the lockdowns!

End the lockdowns! End the lockdowns! Wuhan residents: What do we want? Freedom! Wuhan: Long live democracy! Long live freedom! Chinese people need to be brave! "Shanghai tank man" blocks a police vehicle.

Shanghai residents rallying on Urumuqi Rd: We stand with the people of Xinjiang! We also stand with Iranian women! Shanghai residents: Zero COVID, f*ck you! Xi Jinping, step down! Xi Jinping, step down! Xi Jinping, your mom is dead!

Chengdu: Give me liberty, or give me death! Chengdu: We don't want a chairman for life! Chengdu: China needs no emperor! Beijing: We want universal values! We want freedom, democracy, equality, and the rule of law!

Beijing: End the Cultural Revolution 2.0! Beijing: The Chinese people want human rights and democracy! People in Beijing standing with those in Shanghai. Freedom! Freedom! Freedom! Qinghua University student: From this day forward, I won't ever suck the CCP's d*ck again!

Qinghua Uni student: If we were to remain silent out of fear... the Chinese people would be ashamed of us university students. As a student of Qinghua University, I would regret for the rest of my life. Tianjin: NO to lockdowns! YES to freedom! NO to lies! YES to dignity!

NO to Cultural Revolution! YES to reform! NO to supreme leader! YES to election ballots! NO to slavery! YES to citizenship! Chengdu: Freedom of speech! Freedom of speech! Xi'an: Democracy! Rule of law! Freedom of expression!

Guangzhou: Freedom of speech! Freedom of the press! Dali, Yunnan singing The Internationale. Sichuan Music College reciting The Six Principles of New Youth. White paper protests across China.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ynxswe9cY3o
